FOR ANY RESPONSIVE DOCUMENT WITHHELD ON THE BASIS OF PRIVILEGE, DEFENDANT GEISE SHALL SERVE A PRIVILEGE LOG THAT IDENTIFIES THE NATURE OF THE DOCUMENT, DATE, AUTHOR, RECIPIENTS, BATES NUMBERS, AND THE PRIVILEGE CLAIMED. FOR REQUEST NO. 2, PLAINTIFFS SEEK INFORMATION AND DOCUMENTS PERTAINING TO THE ALTER EGO ALLEGATIONS. THIS INFORMATION IS PROTECTED BY THE FINANCIAL PRIVACY PRIVILEGE. DEFENDANTS GEISE AND COUP D'ETAT HAVE AVERRED THAT PLAINTIFFS' CLAIMS ARE FULLY COVERED BY DEFENDANTS' GENERAL LIABILITY POLICY AND, UNDER THIS POLICY, THEIR INSURER HAS ACCEPTED TENDER OF THE DEFENSE AND INDEMNITY IN THIS ACTION. IN LIGHT OF THIS AVERMENT, DEFENDANT GEISE'S PRIVACY INTERESTS CURRENTLY OUTWEIGHS PLAINTIFFS NEED TO CONDUCT DISCOVERY IN SUPPORT OF THEIR ALTER EGO ALLEGATIONS. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Alter Ego Claims From Pltfs' Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-20-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Allen Kent, Esq., 415.272.2662, for Plaintiffs Helen Horner and John Tobeler.
Michael Pursell, Esq., 415.986.5900, for Defendants Darin Geise and Coup D'Etat, Inc.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS DARIN GEISE and COUP D'ETAT, INC.'S Motion To Strike Alter Ego Claims From Plaintiffs' Second Amended Complaint is denied. Paragraphs 8-10 plead sufficient facts to support an alter ego theory of liability. While the 2nd Amended Complaint does not expressly allege that the corporation is undercapitalized, plaintiff implicitly makes that allegation by alleging that the corporation is a mere shell (par. 8 of the 2nd Amended Complaint). The Court liberally construes plaintiff's allegations pursuant to CCP 452. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976

FREDDIE REPPOND OF MERRILL CORPORATION, TEL. NO. 357-4300, COURT REPORTER.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS HELGA HORNER and JOHN TOBELER's Motion For An Order Compelling Defendant Coup D'Etat, Inc. To Further Respond To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, Numbers 1-32 And 35-48 is Denied. Each request seeks information pertaining to the alter ego allegations. This information is protected by the financial privacy privilege. Defendants Geise and Coup d'Etat have averred that Plaintiffs' claims are fully covered by Defendants' general liability policy and, under this policy, their insurer has accepted tender of the defense and indemnity in this action. In light of this averment, Defendant Geise's privacy interests currently outweighs Plaintiffs need to conduct discovery in support of their alter ego allegations. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Freddie Reppond

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Compelling Deft Darin Geise Dba Coup D'Etat To Provide Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, Numbers 1,2,3,4 And 5; Proof Of Service
Off Calendar Feb-07-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DENNIS C. BIRKHIMER & ATTY. ALLEN J. KENT, TEL. NO. 257-4700 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MICHAEL A. PURSELL OF GORDON & REES LLP, TEL. NO. 875-4271 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS;
FREDDIE REPPOND OF MERRILL CORPORATION, TEL. NO. 357-4300, COURT REPORTER.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFFS HELGA HORNER AND JOHN TOBELER'S Motion For An Order Compelling Defendant Darin Geise Dba Coup D'Etat To Provide Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, Numbers 1,2,3,4 And 5 - Granted as to Request nos. 1, 3, 4 & 5. All preliminary objections to the Request for Production are overruled. Denied as to Request no. 2 without prejudice. For Request nos. 1, 3, 4 and 5, within 10 days of service of notice of entry of order, Defendant Geise shall provide amended verified responses either averring that all responsive documents have been produced or state with particularity which documents have been withheld based on an objection. For any responsive document withheld on the basis of privilege, Defendant Geise shall serve a privilege log that identifies the nature of the document, date, author, recipients, Bates numbers, and the privilege claimed. For Request no. 2, Plaintiffs seek information and documents pertaining to the alter ego allegations. This information is protected by the financial privacy privilege. Defendants Geise and Coup d'Etat have averred that Plaintiffs' claims are fully covered by Defendants' general liability policy and, under this policy, their insurer has accepted tender of the defense and indemnity in this action. In light of this averment, Defendant Geise's privacy interests currently outweighs Plaintiffs need to conduct discovery in support of their alter ego allegations. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Freddie Reppond.
